questions.questionText: What do I do about a bleeding pregnant woman?
options: Fetal contraction monitoring, vital signs, bleeding, pain assessment, abdominal assessment
questions.correctAnswer: Fetal contraction monitoring, vital signs, bleeding, pain assessment, abdominal assessment

questions.questionText: What are interventions for a bleeding pregnant woman?
options: IV placement, IV fluids, labwork, corticosteriods (matures fetal lungs for early delivery), modified bedrest, education, emotional support, prepare for delivery
questions.correctAnswer: IV placement, IV fluids, labwork, corticosteriods (matures fetal lungs for early delivery), modified bedrest, education, emotional support, prepare for delivery

options: Maternal Factors: diabetes, chronic hypertension, preeclampsia, smoking, advanced maternal age (35+).  Fetal Factors: fetal compromise, polyhydramnios (too much amniotic fluid), oligohydramnios (too little amniotic fluid), birth defects, multifetal gestation.
questions.correctAnswer: Maternal Factors: diabetes, chronic hypertension, preeclampsia, smoking, advanced maternal age (35+).  Fetal Factors: fetal compromise, polyhydramnios (too much amniotic fluid), oligohydramnios (too little amniotic fluid), birth defects, multifetal gestation.

questions.explanationText: REMEMBER: painless dilation!  Treatment is placing a suture to pull it shut, until 37 weeks.

questions.questionText: What is a fetal fibronectin test?
options: Biochemical marker for diagnosing preterm labor.
questions.correctAnswer: Biochemical marker for diagnosing preterm labor.
questions.explanationText: If issues, fetal fibronectin is part of vaginal secretions.  We want a negative test.  Positive test indicates preterm birth.

questions.questionText: What is a normal labor contraction pattern?
How do you stop preterm labor?
options: Tocolytics (medications to stop progression of labor after contractions and cervical change have occurred).  Examples: Magnesium sulfate (short term use - delays birth for 48 hours by relaxing uterus - assess for deep tendon reflexes), Nifedipine (Procardia - a calcium channel blockers that relaxes uterus smooth muscle), Terbutaline (Brethine - relaxes smooth muscles, monitor HR + FHR, S.E.: tachycardia), Betamethasone (Celestone - corticosteriod stimulates fetal lung maturity)
questions.correctAnswer: Tocolytics (medications to stop progression of labor after contractions and cervical change have occurred).  Examples: Magnesium sulfate (short term use - delays birth for 48 hours by relaxing uterus - assess for deep tendon reflexes), Nifedipine (Procardia - a calcium channel blockers that relaxes uterus smooth muscle), Terbutaline (Brethine - relaxes smooth muscles, monitor HR + FHR, S.E.: tachycardia), Betamethasone (Celestone - corticosteriod stimulates fetal lung maturity)

questions.questionText: When would tocolytic therapy be contraindicated?
options: If there is no longer a fetal heart beat.  If there is an infection (baby + mom sepsis risk).  Abruption (hemorraging).

questions.questionText: How do you manage preterm labor?
options: Not strict bedrest. Limit work. Restrict sex. Child/homecare help. Support groups. Reading materials.
questions.correctAnswer: Not strict bedrest. Limit work. Restrict sex. Child/homecare help. Support groups. Reading materials.

options: Infection of amniotic fluid that can effect mother and baby.
questions.correctAnswer: Infection of amniotic fluid that can effect mother and baby.

options: A corticosteriod that matures fetal lungs in preterm births.
questions.correctAnswer: A corticosteriod that matures fetal lungs in preterm births.

questions.questionText: What would fetal heart rate be if there is an infection
questions.explanationText: Tachycardia is fetal heart rate greater than 160.

options: Higher risk for anemia, placenta previa/abruption, premature labor, two-vessel cords, higher rates of surgical births.
questions.correctAnswer: Higher risk for anemia, placenta previa/abruption, premature labor, two-vessel cords, higher rates of surgical births.

options: Excessive vomiting causing weight loss, eletrolyte imablance, nutritional deficiencies, and ketouria.
questions.correctAnswer: Excessive vomiting causing weight loss, eletrolyte imablance, nutritional deficiencies, and ketouria.
questions.explanationText: Begins within first 10 weeks of pregnancy. Resolves by 20 weeks.
